flags.concern: Timeline indicator typing: Step 3 fixes `isNewTrackTop`, but it still leaves `DropIndicator.tsx` reading `position.trackId` without declaring or forwarding that field.
flags.evidence: `DropIndicatorPosition` currently ends at `newTrackKind`/`reject` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:23-27`, but `DropIndicator` reads `position?.trackId` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:68-77`. Upstream, `toIndicatorPosition()` still only forwards `isNewTrack`, `newTrackKind`, and `reject` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:64-80`. The revised plan only adds `isNewTrackTop`, so the strict-TS mismatch around `trackId` remains unplanned.

flags.concern: Workspace hygiene: Step 2 still uses `git checkout HEAD -- <file>` against dirty files even though the repo still has unstaged edits in those paths and the plan itself keeps an open question about why the revert exists.
flags.evidence: Current repo state still shows unstaged modifications in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx` (`git status --short`). The revised plan's Step 2 would overwrite six of those files before resolving whether the revert was intentional.
